title: artemisia maritima subsp. gallica
intro: Artemisia maritima subsp. gallica, also known as Gallic Sea Wormwood, is a subspecies of the Artemisia maritima plant, which is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Gallic Sea Wormwood include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
